member.name: T:Scalar.AspNetCore.Annotations.Attributes.ScalarBadgeAttribute
member.summary: Represents an attribute for adding badges to API endpoints in the Scalar UI.

member.name: P:Scalar.AspNetCore.Annotations.Attributes.ScalarBadgeAttribute.Name
member.summary: Specifies the text to be displayed on the badge in the Scalar UI. The badge name serves as a label or identifier for the badge, providing users with a clear and concise description of the badge's purpose or significance in the context of the API documentation. By setting the name property, you can effectively communicate important information or categorization about specific API endpoints, enhancing the overall clarity and usability of your API documentation in the Scalar UI.

member.name: P:Scalar.AspNetCore.Annotations.Attributes.ScalarBadgeAttribute.Position
member.summary: Specifies the position of the badge in the Scalar UI. The badge can be positioned either before or after the operation header, allowing for flexible placement based on the desired visual hierarchy and emphasis. By default, the badge is positioned after the operation header, but you can choose to position it before if you want to give it more prominence or make it stand out more in the UI. This property allows you to control the layout and presentation of badges in the Scalar UI, enhancing the visual organization and user experience of your API documentation.

member.name: P:Scalar.AspNetCore.Annotations.Attributes.ScalarBadgeAttribute.Color
member.summary: Specifies the color of the badge in the Scalar UI. The color can be defined using a custom hex color code (e.g., "#FF5733") or a predefined theme string recognized by Scalar (e.g., "primary", "secondary", "success", "danger", etc.). By setting the color property, you can visually differentiate badges based on their significance, category, or any other criteria you choose, making it easier for users to quickly identify and understand the context of the badge in the API documentation. This customization option allows you to enhance the visual appeal and clarity of your API documentation in the Scalar UI.

member.name: M:Scalar.AspNetCore.Annotations.Attributes.ScalarBadgeAttribute.#ctor(System.String,BadgePosition,System.String)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class with the specified badge name, position, and color. This constructor allows you to annotate an API endpoint with a custom badge that can be displayed in the Scalar UI, providing additional visual cues or information about the endpoint. The badge name is a required parameter that specifies the text to be displayed on the badge, while the position and color parameters are optional and allow you to customize the appearance and placement of the badge in the UI. By using this attribute, you can enhance the visibility and context of specific API endpoints in the generated OpenAPI documentation for Scalar-based APIs.

member.name: T:Scalar.AspNetCore.Annotations.Attributes.ScalarExcludeAttribute
member.summary: Represents an attribute that can be applied to API endpoints to indicate that they should be excluded from the Scalar UI. When this attribute is present on a method, it serves as a marker to hide the corresponding endpoint from being displayed in the Scalar UI, while still keeping it accessible in the underlying OpenAPI JSON documentation. This allows developers to control the visibility of specific API endpoints in the Scalar UI without affecting their availability in the API documentation or functionality. By using this attribute, you can effectively manage which endpoints are showcased in the Scalar UI, providing a cleaner and more focused user experience for API consumers.

member.name: T:Scalar.AspNetCore.Annotations.Attributes.ScalarOperationAttribute
member.summary: Enriches the OpenAPI Operation with Scalar-specific UI metadata.

member.name: P:Scalar.AspNetCore.Annotations.Attributes.ScalarOperationAttribute.Summary
member.summary: A short summary of what the operation does.

member.name: P:Scalar.AspNetCore.Annotations.Attributes.ScalarOperationAttribute.Description
member.summary: A verbose explanation of the operation behavior.

member.name: P:Scalar.AspNetCore.Annotations.Attributes.ScalarOperationAttribute.OperationId
member.summary: The unique string used to identify the operation.

member.name: P:Scalar.AspNetCore.Annotations.Attributes.ScalarOperationAttribute.Tags
member.summary: Overrides the default tags for grouping in the Scalar UI.

member.name: P:Scalar.AspNetCore.Annotations.Attributes.ScalarOperationAttribute.ThemeColor
member.summary: A custom hex color or theme string applied to this specific endpoint in the Scalar UI.

member.name: T:Scalar.AspNetCore.Annotations.Attributes.ScalarResponseAttribute
member.summary: Marker attribute to indicate that the response of an API endpoint should be treated as a "Scalar response" in the context of API documentation generation. This attribute can be used by tools like Swashbuckle to apply specific transformations or enhancements to the OpenAPI documentation for endpoints that return Scalar responses, such as adding custom extensions, modifying schema representations, or applying special formatting rules.

member.name: M:Scalar.AspNetCore.Annotations.Attributes.ScalarResponseAttribute.#ctor(System.Int32,System.String)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class with the specified HTTP status code and an optional description. This constructor allows you to annotate an API endpoint with a specific response status code and provide additional context or information about the response using the description parameter, which can be utilized by API documentation generators to enhance the generated OpenAPI documentation for endpoints that return Scalar responses.

member.name: M:Scalar.AspNetCore.Annotations.Attributes.ScalarResponseAttribute.#ctor(System.Type,System.Int32,Scalar.AspNetCore.Annotations.Enums.ScalarContentType[])
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class with the specified response type and HTTP status code. This constructor allows you to annotate an API endpoint with a specific response type (e.g., a model class) and an associated HTTP status code, indicating that the endpoint returns a Scalar response of the specified type. This information can be leveraged by API documentation generators to enhance the OpenAPI documentation for endpoints that return Scalar responses, providing more detailed information about the expected response schema and status code.

member.name: M:Scalar.AspNetCore.Annotations.Attributes.ScalarResponseAttribute.#ctor(System.Type,System.Int32,Scalar.AspNetCore.Annotations.Enums.ScalarContentType,System.String,Scalar.AspNetCore.Annotations.Enums.ScalarContentType[])
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class with the specified response type, HTTP status code, primary content type, and additional content types. This constructor allows you to annotate an API endpoint with a specific response type, an associated HTTP status code, and multiple content types that the endpoint can produce in its response. This information can be utilized by API documentation generators to enhance the OpenAPI documentation for endpoints that return Scalar responses by providing detailed information about the expected response schema, status code, and the various formats in which the response can be provided.

member.name: M:Scalar.AspNetCore.Annotations.Attributes.ScalarResponseAttribute.#ctor
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class with the specified HTTP status code. This constructor allows you to annotate an API endpoint with a specific response status code, indicating that the endpoint returns a Scalar response with that status code. This information can be leveraged by API documentation generators to enhance the OpenAPI documentation for endpoints that return Scalar responses by providing information about the expected response status code.

member.name: P:Scalar.AspNetCore.Annotations.Attributes.ScalarResponseAttribute.Description
member.summary: Gets or sets a description for the response, providing additional context or information about the response that can be utilized by API documentation generators to enhance the generated OpenAPI documentation for endpoints that return Scalar responses. This property allows you to provide a textual description of the response, which can help clarify the purpose or meaning of the response in the context of the API documentation.

member.name: P:Scalar.AspNetCore.Annotations.Attributes.ScalarResponseAttribute.ContentTypes
member.summary: Gets or sets an array of additional content types that the API endpoint can produce in its response. This property allows you to specify multiple content types (e.g., "application/json", "application/xml") that the endpoint can return, which can be utilized by API documentation generators to enhance the OpenAPI documentation for endpoints that return Scalar responses by indicating the various formats in which the response can be provided.

member.name: T:Scalar.AspNetCore.Annotations.Attributes.ScalarSchemaAttribute
member.summary: Enriches the OpenAPI Schema for requests/responses with mock data and descriptions.

member.name: P:Scalar.AspNetCore.Annotations.Attributes.ScalarSchemaAttribute.Description
member.summary: A description of the property or class.

member.name: P:Scalar.AspNetCore.Annotations.Attributes.ScalarSchemaAttribute.Format
member.summary: A specific format for the string (e.g., "date-time", "uuid", "email").

member.name: P:Scalar.AspNetCore.Annotations.Attributes.ScalarSchemaAttribute.MockValue
member.summary: A realistic mock value used by the Scalar UI API client.

member.name: P:Scalar.AspNetCore.Annotations.Attributes.ScalarSchemaAttribute.ReadOnly
member.summary: Marks the property as read-only (not expected in requests).

member.name: P:Scalar.AspNetCore.Annotations.Attributes.ScalarSchemaAttribute.WriteOnly
member.summary: Marks the property as write-only (hidden in responses, e.g., passwords).

member.name: T:Scalar.AspNetCore.Annotations.Enums.ScalarContentType
member.summary: Represents standard content types used in HTTP APIs.

member.name: T:Scalar.AspNetCore.Annotations.Swashbuckle.ScalarSwashbuckleExtensions
member.summary: Provides extension methods for integrating Scalar-specific annotations into the Swashbuckle OpenAPI generator pipeline. This class contains methods that can be called on the SwaggerGenOptions to add custom operation and schema filters that recognize Scalar attributes and enhance the generated API documentation accordingly, allowing for features like badges, code samples, and enhanced schema descriptions in the Scalar UI.

member.name: T:BadgePosition
member.summary: Defines the possible positions for badges in the Scalar UI, allowing developers to specify whether a badge should be displayed before or after the operation header in the generated API documentation. This enumeration provides a clear and standardized way to control the placement of badges, enhancing the visual organization and user experience of API documentation in the Scalar UI.

member.name: F:BadgePosition.After
member.summary: Position the badge after the operation header (default).

member.name: F:BadgePosition.Before
member.summary: Position the badge before the operation header.
